inline_review:
commit 2377c60078ee617bb248930464364777015e5fe2
Author: SeongJae Park <sj@kernel.org>

mm/damon/core: avoid use of half-online-committed context

This commit introduces a maybe_corrupted flag to avoid using a DAMON context
that was partially updated due to an internal allocation failure in
damon_commit_ctx(). When this flag is set, kdamond ignores remaining callbacks
and terminates.

Link: https://lore.kernel.org/20260319043309.97966-1-sj@kernel.org

> diff --git a/mm/damon/core.c b/mm/damon/core.c
> index c1d1091d307e..37454e8c9c51 100644
> --- a/mm/damon/core.c
> +++ b/mm/damon/core.c
> @@ -1252,6 +1252,7 @@ int damon_commit_ctx(struct damon_ctx *dst, struct damon_ctx *src)
>  {
>  	int err;
>  
> +	dst->maybe_corrupted = true;
>  	if (!is_power_of_2(src->min_region_sz))
>  		return -EINVAL;

Could this prematurely mark the context as corrupted? 

If the power-of-2 validation fails, the function returns -EINVAL without
modifying dst at all. However, because maybe_corrupted is already set to
true, wouldn't the kdamond thread assume its state is corrupted and 
unconditionally shut down?

Should the flag be set immediately prior to the first mutating operation
like damon_commit_schemes() instead?

[ ... ]

> @@ -2678,6 +2680,8 @@ static void kdamond_call(struct damon_ctx *ctx, bool cancel)
>  			complete(&control->completion);
>  		else if (control->canceled && control->dealloc_on_cancel)
>  			kfree(control);
> +		if (ctx->maybe_corrupted)
> +			break;
>  	}

Does this early break cause a deadlock during cancellation?

When damon_commit_ctx() fails, it sets ctx->maybe_corrupted = true. 
This corruption triggers an exit from the main loop in kdamond_fn() to the 
done: label, which invokes kdamond_call(ctx, true) to cancel and complete
all remaining pending callbacks.

However, inside kdamond_call(), this loop now unconditionally checks
if (ctx->maybe_corrupted) break; at the end of each iteration. Because
the flag is true, won't the loop process exactly one callback and then
break, leaving the rest of the callbacks in ctx->call_controls unprocessed?

If the worker thread then exits, wouldn't any callers waiting on 
wait_for_completion(&control->completion) for those remaining callbacks
hang indefinitely?

Would it make sense to bypass this break condition when cancel is true?
output:
{"findings":[{"problem":"kdamond_call() loop breaks early during cancellation when maybe_corrupted is true, leading to deadlocks for threads waiting on remaining callbacks.","severity":"High","severity_explanation":"1. If `damon_commit_ctx()` fails, it sets `ctx->maybe_corrupted = true`.
2. In `kdamond_fn()`, this corruption triggers an exit from the main loop to the `done:` label, where `kdamond_call(ctx, true)` is invoked to cancel and complete all remaining pending callbacks.
3. However, inside `kdamond_call()`, the loop checks `if (ctx->maybe_corrupted) break;` unconditionally at the end of each iteration.
4. Because `maybe_corrupted` is true, the loop will process exactly one callback and then break, leaving the rest of the callbacks in `ctx->call_controls` unprocessed and their completions unsignaled.
5. Since the worker thread then exits, any callers waiting on `wait_for_completion(&control->completion)` for those remaining callbacks will hang indefinitely, resulting in a permanent deadlock. The break condition should likely be bypassed when `cancel` is true."},{"problem":"maybe_corrupted is set to true prematurely before any actual state modification occurs, which can cause unnecessary thread termination.","severity":"Low","severity_explanation":"1. In `damon_commit_ctx()`, `dst->maybe_corrupted = true;` is executed before the parameter validation check `if (!is_power_of_2(src->min_region_sz))`.
2. If the validation fails, the function returns `-EINVAL` without making any modifications to `dst`.
3. However, because the flag was already set, the kdamond thread will assume its state is corrupted and unconditionally shut down.
4. Even though callers typically pre-validate parameters (as noted in the commit message), marking the context as corrupted before any actual mutation begins is logically incorrect and fragile. The flag should be set immediately prior to the first mutating operation (e.g., `damon_commit_schemes`)."}],"fixes":"","review_inline":"commit 2377c60078ee617bb248930464364777015e5fe2
